slots.rod: RIVERTEX Fenix 11'5" (Match Rod, 6.5-17.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Fluoro leader provides stealth for bass in the clear Everglades channels. Small Minnows on #4/0 targets the predator species. However, the Fenix's 17.5 Lb max line weight may be tested by Common Snook (46.3 lb) or Red Drum (47.4 lb) if they take the bait.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-6 ft. Everglades channels are often shallow with heavy vegetation. At Lily Pad Channel, try 3-4 ft near lily pads. At The Salty Delta, go deeper (5-7 ft) where larger predators cruise.

issues: Rod/reel is undergunned for the Everglades monsters — Common Snook (46.3 lb), Red Drum (47.4 lb), and Tarpon (33.1 lb) will snap 17.5 Lb line easily
Small Minnows work for bass and gar but NOT for the highest-value targets (Gafftopsail Catfish, Warmouth)
Good for Butterfly Peacock Bass ($150/kg) and Largemouth Bass — stick to these targets with this setup
improvements: This is a solid bass/gar float rig — don't try to fight the monster fish with it
Small Minnows + #4/0 targets Butterfly Peacock Bass ($150/kg, 10.8 lb max) — your best earner on this rod
If a Common Snook or Red Drum takes the bait, loosen the drag immediately and play it very carefully
Consider switching to Shiners for broader coverage — adds Bowfin and Common Snook to your float targets
Deploy at Lily Pad Channel where Peacock Bass hold near vegetation

slots.rod: JigWinner 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast)
issues: Light rod (3-13 Lb) is severely undergunned for Everglades monsters
Tarpon (33.1 lb), Common Snook (46.3 lb), and Red Drum (47.4 lb) will overpower this setup
Titanium leader protects against gar teeth but won't save you from a drag-screaming Snook run
Turtle Spoon 1/4 Oz is good for bass-sized fish but may be too small for the big predators
improvements: Good for Butterfly Peacock Bass and Largemouth Bass — stay in the channels where these species hold
If a Tarpon or Snook hits, set drag very loose and be prepared for a long fight
Consider upgrading to a medium or medium-heavy rod for Everglades — this location has much bigger fish than previous locations
Titanium leader is essential here — Florida Gar will bite through anything else
Cast near structure and mangrove roots where Peacock Bass ambush prey

slots.rod: Loki 7'6" SE (Spinning Rod, Heavy, 5/7-2 1/3 Oz, 6-24 Lb, Moderate)
slots.terminal_tackle: Jighead 1/2 Oz. #2/0 with Worm 3" soft plastic
issues: Heavy rod with 6-24 Lb line is actually WELL-SUITED for Everglades — this was overpowered at other locations but perfect here
Worm 3" effectively targets both bass species and can attract Snook and Red Drum
1/2 Oz jig works well in the shallow-to-moderate Everglades channels
improvements: This slot SHINES at the Everglades — the Heavy rod finally matches the fish size available here
Worm 3" jig is effective for Butterfly Peacock Bass and Largemouth Bass
The 24 Lb line gives you a fighting chance against Common Snook and Red Drum if they strike
Titanium leader protects against Florida Gar and toothy predators
Work the jig near submerged structure and mangrove roots — hop it along bottom in channels
This is your best shot at landing the monster fish — Snook, Red Drum, even Tarpon could hit a jig

leader_recommendation.why: Fluoro leader for panfish. Semolina Balls attract Bluegill and Golden Shiner but coverage is very limited at the Everglades. Many higher-value species are available.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 2-4 ft in shallow backwaters and channels where panfish hold.

issues: Durability critically low (15446/18000) — rod is at risk of breaking
Semolina Balls only covers Bluegill ($70/kg) and Golden Shiner ($70/kg) — very low-value targets at a $6,500 travel location
The 3-6.5 Lb line will snap instantly if any Everglades predator takes the bait
This rod is wasted potential at the Everglades — 13 higher-value species available
improvements: Switch bait to Crickets — covers Bluegill, Warmouth ($170/kg!), and Redear Sunfish ($120/kg)
Warmouth at $170/kg is the HIGHEST $/kg fish at Everglades and is caught with Crickets/Grasshoppers/Red Worms
Even better: switch to Bloodworms or Red Worms to add Oscar ($120/kg) coverage
URGENT: Repair this rod before the trip — a break at $6,500 travel cost is expensive
Deploy on RodPod Trio in a protected backwater for passive Warmouth/panfish income

slots.rod: Rivertex Zeus 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, X-Heavy, 3/4-2 1/8 Oz)
slots.leader: Fluorocarbon Leader .033" (79 Lb) — swap to Titanium .005" for toothy fish
slots.terminal_tackle: Heavy spoon, crankbait, or jig — match to target species
issues: None — this is the correct setup for Everglades monster fish
Note: At 81.5 Lb drag and 41.2 Lb line, your weakest link is the line. Set drag to ~30 Lb for a safe margin
improvements: MONSTER FISH SETUP — Zeus SE + L'Impulsion 12000 SE is your strongest spinning combo from inventory
81.5 Lb max drag handles Red Drum (47.4 lb) at ~58% tension — comfortable fight vs the 92% you had before
Common Snook (46.3 lb) and Tarpon (33.1 lb) become manageable catches instead of rod-breaking emergencies
41.2 Lb braid gives plenty of strength with thin diameter for good casting distance
79 Lb fluoro leader provides abrasion resistance against structure; swap to titanium .005" for gar teeth
Replaces the old Aurora Light setup that was overlapping with slot 2 and undergunned for big fish
Use heavy crankbaits (you have 46 in inventory) for Snook/Red Drum at The Salty Delta
Use heavy spoons or spinnerbaits for Tarpon at Lily Pad Channel
This rod also handles Florida Gar (22.1 lb) and Bowfin (11 lb) with ease

slots.rod: ValueSpin 6'3" (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-6.5 Lb, Fast)
issues: 3-6.5 Lb line is extremely light for Everglades — many fish here exceed 10 lb
Nano Spinner is small for the predator-heavy Everglades species
Any hookup with Snook, Red Drum, Tarpon, or even larger Peacock Bass will break this off
improvements: Use for panfish-sized targets only — Bluegill, small Peacock Bass, Oscar
The short 6'3" rod is good for tight casting in mangrove tunnels at Infinity Tunnels
Titanium leader protects the setup even if undergunned fish-wise
Consider a heavier spinning setup for one of slots 5 or 6 when you unlock higher-level gear

slots.rod: MCT McCarp 13'2" (Match Rod, 9-25.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Fluoro leader for panfish/sunfish in clear Everglades water. Red Worms target Warmouth ($170/kg!) which is the highest price-per-kg fish here. The McCarp's 25.5 Lb line gives some insurance if a bass takes the bait.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-5 ft. Warmouth hold in shallow vegetated areas and near structure. At The Floating Thickets, try 3-4 ft near thick vegetation where Warmouth ambush prey.

issues: Hook #6 is good for Warmouth and panfish but limits predator catches
Red Worms cover Warmouth, Bluegill, and Pumpkinseed-sized fish but NOT crappie or Oscar specifically
Red Worms ARE effective for Warmouth — this is a good match
improvements: GREAT bait choice — Red Worms target Warmouth ($170/kg!), the highest $/kg fish at Everglades
Warmouth max weight is 1.8 lb at $170/kg = solid income for a small fish
Consider switching to Bloodworms for even broader coverage — adds Oscar ($120/kg) and Redear Sunfish ($120/kg)
Deploy on RodPod Trio at The Floating Thickets for passive Warmouth income while actively bass fishing
The McCarp's strong backbone can handle surprise bass hookups better than the OmniFloat

gear.boat: Garry Scott Lupus Motor Boat (NOT USABLE — no boat fishing at Everglades)
notes: Lupus Motor Boat is equipped but Everglades does NOT allow boat fishing — shore fishing only
Luminox Headlamp — essential for night sessions. Many Everglades species are more active after dark
RodPod Trio — deploy in shallow backwaters for passive Warmouth/panfish income
FishStronghold M Plus Keepnet — fill with Peacock Bass and Warmouth for maximum income
Groundbait is not essential for Everglades — most target fish are predators or taken on live bait
Could concentrate panfish in backwater areas if pursuing Warmouth or Bluegill

passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Thick vegetation provides cover that concentrates Warmouth ($170/kg), Bluegill, and Redear Sunfish. The shallow backwater areas here are protected from current, keeping floats stable. Nearby deeper channels hold bass and gar for active fishing.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Cast spinning rods (slots 2, 3, 5) into adjacent channels and along mangrove edges within sight of your rod stand. Lily Pad Channel and Infinity Tunnels are nearby for active bass hunting. Stay close enough to react to float bites.
strategy: Set RodPod Trio at The Floating Thickets — deploy float rods in shallow vegetated water
Slot 4 (OmniFloat + Semolina Balls): Cast to 2-3 ft near vegetation — SWITCH TO CRICKETS for Warmouth ($170/kg)
Slot 7 (McCarp + Red Worms + #6): Cast to 3-4 ft near structure for Warmouth and Redear Sunfish
Slot 1 (Fenix + Small Minnows + #4/0): Cast to 4-6 ft targeting Peacock Bass and Largemouth Bass passively
Actively fish slot 5 (Zeus SE + L'Impulsion 12000) for Snook/Red Drum/Tarpon — this is your MONSTER FISH rod now
Slot 3 (Loki Heavy + Worm jig) is your secondary active rod for bass and mid-size predators
Warmouth bite aggressively — small, quick float dips. Set hook immediately.
CAUTION: Everglades has monster fish — if a Red Drum (47.4 lb) or Tarpon (33.1 lb) takes a float rod, grab it FAST and loosen drag
Night sessions: Keep float rods soaking — Brown Bullhead and Channel Catfish become active after dark
Sell keepnet when full of Warmouth + Peacock Bass — these are your money fish per trip
income_estimate: Warmouth ($170/kg, max 1.8 lb) — highest $/kg at Everglades. Passive float rods catch 3-5/hr
Butterfly Peacock Bass ($150/kg, max 10.8 lb) — slot 1 passive + slots 2,3,5 active = $150-750 per catch
Gafftopsail Catfish ($152/kg, max 9.5 lb) — bonus catches at The Salty Delta with Shrimp/Crawfish bait
Passive Warmouth income: ~$170/kg x 0.5kg avg x 4 catches/hr = ~$340/hr from stand rods
Active bass income: ~$150/kg x 3kg avg x 3 catches/hr = ~$1,350/hr from spinning
Combined: $1,500-$2,500 per in-game hour with optimized setup
WARNING: Travel cost is $6,500 + $1,000/day extend — plan for 2-3 day trips to recoup investment

missing_setups: Bowfin now covered by slot 5 Zeus combo with heavy spoons/jigs — also responds to slot 1 Small Minnows if Shiners equipped
Gafftopsail Catfish needs Shrimp, Crawfish Cut, Crawfish, or Small Cutbait — none equipped. Bring Shrimp for The Salty Delta sessions.
Oscar needs Marshmallows, Semolina Balls, Red Worms, or Bloodworms — slot 4 with Semolina Balls covers this IF you fish near Oscar habitat
Redear Sunfish needs Blood, Maggots, Crickets, Wax Worms, or Bloodworms — switch slot 4 to Bloodworms/Crickets
Brown Bullhead needs Marshmallows, Blood, Shrimp, Leeches, or Small Cutbait — night bait swap opportunity
Channel Catfish needs Pet Food, Leeches, Small Cutbait, Large Cutbait, or Blood — need dedicated bottom rig
xp_optimization: 15 species available — catch ALL for massive first-catch XP bonuses
Unique Butterfly Peacock Bass, Common Snook, Florida Gar, Gafftopsail Catfish, and Largemouth Bass give huge first-catch bonuses
Trophy Common Snook (46.3 lb!) and Red Drum (47.4 lb!) give enormous XP — slot 5 Zeus combo can now handle these fights comfortably
Fish all 4 spots for location discovery XP — Infinity Tunnels, Lily Pad Channel, The Floating Thickets, The Salty Delta
Night fishing unlocks catfish species — Brown Bullhead and Channel Catfish are night feeders
Challenge catches on light tackle (slots 2, 6) give bonus XP on bass — slot 5 is now your power rod
income_optimization: Warmouth at $170/kg is the best $/kg fish — target passively with float rods and Red Worms/Crickets
Gafftopsail Catfish at $152/kg (max 9.5 lb) — bring Shrimp to unlock this income at The Salty Delta
Butterfly Peacock Bass at $150/kg (max 10.8 lb) — up to $735 per trophy! Target with spoons and jigs
Oscar at $120/kg and Redear Sunfish at $120/kg — good passive income with Bloodworms/Crickets
Tarpon at $100/kg (max 33.1 lb) — up to $1,500 per fish! Zeus SE + L'Impulsion can handle these now
WARNING: Everglades is expensive ($6,500 travel + $1,000/day). Focus on high-value species to profit.
Fill keepnet with Warmouth + Peacock Bass + Gafftopsail Catfish before selling — maximize income per trip
priority_changes: CRITICAL: Switch slot 4 bait from Semolina Balls to Crickets or Bloodworms — unlocks Warmouth ($170/kg) and Redear Sunfish ($120/kg)
IMPORTANT: Bring Shrimp bait — unlocks Gafftopsail Catfish ($152/kg) and adds Common Snook/Red Drum/Tarpon coverage on float rigs
DONE: Slot 5 upgraded to Zeus SE + L'Impulsion 12000 SE — monster fish are now handleable at ~58% tension instead of 92%
Switch slot 1 bait from Small Minnows to Shiners — adds Bowfin and Common Snook float coverage
Repair OmniFloat (slot 4) before the trip — breaking a rod at $6,500 travel is costly
Slot 5 (Zeus SE) is now your MVP — use for Snook, Red Drum, Tarpon, and Florida Gar. Slot 3 (Loki Heavy) is your secondary power rod for bass.
